itinerary.mapNote: Access via jet boat. NZ's deepest lake. Beech forest. Pure wilderness. 330m.
Muddy Fiordland terrain begins immediately. Track often submerged. 400m.

itinerary.route: Halfway Hut to Loch Maree Hut
itinerary.mapNote: Remote loch camp. No shortcuts, no alternatives, no mobile signal. 400m.

itinerary.route: Loch Maree to Lake Roe Hut
itinerary.mapNote: Alpine crossing. Views finally. Most dramatic section. 600m.
Exposed ridge bivouac. Weather dependent — be prepared to wait. 600m.
Descend to Dusky Sound. Water taxi pre-booked from here. 0m (sea level).
Water taxi to Lake Manapouri, then bus to Te Anau. Civilisation. 0m.

waypoints.name: Lake Hauroko
waypoints.note: New Zealand's deepest lake. Jet boat access. Complete wilderness.

waypoints.name: Dusky Sound
waypoints.note: One of Fiordland's most remote fiords. Accessed only by this trail or boat.

waypoints.name: Loch Maree
waypoints.note: Remote loch deep in the Fiordland interior. No road access ever.

waypoints.name: Mount Seaforth
waypoints.note: Highest point visible from the route. Classic Fiordland dark pyramid.
